subject: Ecology
title: Figs and Fig Wasps
description: A 60-second lesson on Figs and Fig Wasps.

hook.text: Every species of fig tree has its own species of wasp—over 750 matched pairs worldwide.

buildup.text: The female wasp enters the fig, pollinates it, and lays eggs inside—both species reproduce.

discovery.text: This partnership is 80 million years old. Neither can survive without the other.

twist.text: Fig wasps die inside the fig after pollinating. The enzyme ficin breaks down their bodies.

climax.text: Figs are keystone fruits in tropical forests—hundreds of animal species depend on them.

punchline.text: One wasp feeds an entire forest.

quiz.question: How old is the fig-fig wasp partnership?
